mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) consistently grow due to consumer confidence and income, driving corporate earnings expectations upward and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. This signal is always active because consumer spending is a persistent driver of economic growth.
failure_condition: The 20-day forward return is negative in more than 60% of months when RSAFS is above its 12-month moving average, or the rank IC over rolling 3-year windows is consistently below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 5.5% signals labor market weakness that reduces consumer spending and corporate profitability, leading to lower equity prices over the next quarter. The threshold at 5.5% is historically associated with recession alerts.
failure_condition: After UNRATE crosses 5.5%, the subsequent 60-day SPX return is positive in more than 50% of instances, or the average return is not statistically negative at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 3.0% and the CPI index exceeds 180, real interest rates are highly negative, which encourages borrowing and investment in equities. This joint condition amplifies the equity risk premium compression, boosting S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: When the joint condition holds, the 20-day SPX return is negative in more than 55% of instances, or the average excess return over risk-free rate is not positive and significant at 10% level.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) rising above 118 indicates the economy is in an expansion phase beyond the prior cyclical peak, signaling robust supply and corporate earnings. This regime-conditional signal is most powerful during early expansion and produces positive forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: When INDPRO exceeds 118, the 20-day SPX return has a negative median or the proportion of positive returns is below 50% over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A low federal funds rate level (2.28%) directly reduces the discount rate for equities, making them more attractive compared to bonds. This always-on monetary accommodation supports short-term equity returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The 5-day forward return after a low FEDFUNDS observation (below 3%) has a negative average or the signal's Sharpe ratio is below 0 in rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in retail sales (RSAFS) of more than 1% month-over-month signals sudden consumer spending weakness, which directly lowers corporate revenue expectations and triggers negative equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: After a RSAFS decline >1%, the 20-day SPX return is positive in more than 40% of cases, or the average return is not statistically negative at 10% level.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous decline in unemployment rate (UNRATE) and increase in retail sales (RSAFS) over three months reflects a virtuous cycle of employment and consumption that powerfully drives corporate earnings and equity prices. The joint signal is stronger than either series alone.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declining and RSAFS increasing over 3 months
failure_condition: When both conditions hold, the subsequent 60-day SPX return is negative in more than 50% of quarters, or the average return is not significantly positive at 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: In a low inflation environment (CPIAUCSL below 200), a large monthly increase in housing starts (HOUST) signals robust construction and consumer confidence, boosting equity markets over the next 20 days. The regime condition filters out periods when inflation may offset demand gains.
failure_condition: When HOUST increases >2% and CPIAUCSL <200, the 20-day SPX return is negative in more than 50% of occurrences, or the average return is not positive and significant at 10% level.
